title: Merge Sort
description: Implement Merge Sort. Sort an integer array using the merge sort algorithm. Return a pair: [sorted array, number of swaps (inversions)].
key_concept: Divide and conquer — split in half, recursively sort, merge. O(n log n) guaranteed, stable sort, O(n) extra space
when_best: Stable sort needed, external sorting, linked lists (no random access), counting inversions

hints.L1: Split array in half recursively until size 1. Merge two sorted halves by comparing front elements.
hints.L2: merge(left, right): two pointers, pick smaller head. Append remainder. Count: each time right[j] < left[i], add len(left)-i to inversion count.
hints.L3: def merge_sort(arr): if len<=1: return arr,0; mid=len//2; L,c1=merge_sort(arr[:mid]); R,c2=merge_sort(arr[mid:]); merged,c3=merge(L,R); return merged, c1+c2+c3
